memes.label: Abdulin
memes.description: A merchant of the provincial town who supplies the Governor and is later among those petitioning Khlestakov against the Governor's abuses. (P02-025)

memes.label: Ammos Liapkin-Tiapkin
memes.description: The district judge, an elected nobleman who takes bribes in the form of hunting dogs and has never read a legal brief — embodying the corruption of the provincial judiciary in Gogol's satire. (P02-006)

memes.label: Anna and Marya Prepare
memes.description: In the opening scenes of Act III, Anna Andreyevna and her daughter Marya Antonovna anxiously await news of the distinguished guest and frantically prepare themselves to receive him, revealing their vanity and social pretensions.

memes.label: Anna Andreyevna
memes.description: The Governor's wife, a provincial coquette whose social vanity and competition with her daughter for Khlestakov's attention provide the play's sharpest satire of provincial gentility and feminine social ambition. (P03-002)

memes.label: Announcement of the Inspector General
memes.description: The Governor's announcement in Act I, Scene 1 that an inspector general is arriving incognito — the event that sets the entire plot in motion. (002-002)

memes.label: Anton Antonovich
memes.description: The Governor of the provincial town, a career civil servant whose panic at the arrival of an inspector general drives the plot of The Inspector General. (P02-001)

memes.label: Appearance vs. Reality
memes.description: The thematic tension between public appearance and private reality that structures Gogol's comedy. (004-002)

memes.label: Artemy Zemlianika
memes.description: The Superintendent of Charities, a fat man who runs a hospital where patients "recover like flies" and who systematically denounces every colleague to save himself. (P02-007)

memes.label: Asher Smith
memes.description: Production team member credited in colophon

memes.label: Bribery
memes.description: The pervasive practice of taking bribes among Tsarist officials, central to Gogol's satire of bureaucratic corruption in The Inspector General. (P03-001)

memes.label: The Bribery Parade
memes.description: The sequence in Act IV where each town official privately visits Khlestakov to offer a "loan," forming the play's central comic set piece — a complete taxonomy of bureaucratic corruption rendered as farce.

memes.label: Charitable Hospital
memes.description: The charitable hospital administered by Artemy Zemlianika, emblematic of the neglect and corruption in the town's public institutions. (P02-007)

memes.label: Charitable Institutions
memes.description: The hospitals and welfare institutions nominally administered by the Superintendent of Charities in The Inspector General — exposed as sites of catastrophic neglect and bureaucratic theater where patients "recover like flies" and the church was never built. (P02-007)

memes.label: Board of Charities
memes.description: The administrative body overseeing the town's charitable institutions, headed by Artemy Zemlianika. (P02-007)

memes.label: Christiaan Julius Lodewijk Portman
memes.description: Production team member credited in colophon

memes.label: Christian Hubner
memes.description: The German-born district doctor who speaks no Russian, a silent emblem of the provincial administration's dysfunction. (P02-014)

memes.label: Civil Service
memes.description: The Tsarist civil service system and its role in Gogol's satire of provincial bureaucracy in The Inspector General. (P03-001)

memes.label: "Colophon"
memes.description: "Colophon"

memes.label: David Widger
memes.description: Project Gutenberg production team member credited in colophon

memes.label: Deception and Self-Deception
memes.description: The intertwined mechanisms of deception and self-deception that drive the plot of The Inspector General, where the officials are not merely fooled by Khlestakov but actively fool themselves through projection, fear, and the structural pressures of a rank-based society. (P03-001)

memes.label: Derzhimorda
memes.description: A police sergeant known for his excessive use of force, serving alongside Svistunov under Captain Ukhovertov. (P02-023)

memes.label: "Directions for Actors"
memes.description: "Directions for Actors"

memes.label: District Court
memes.description: The district court presided over by Judge Liapkin-Tiapkin, emblematic of the decay of provincial judicial institutions in Gogol's Russia. (P02-006)

memes.label: District Courthouse
memes.description: The district courthouse where Judge Liapkin-Tiapkin presides, notable for the geese wandering through the waiting room and the assessor who smells of vodka. (P02-006)

memes.label: District School
memes.description: The district school overseen by Inspector Luka Khlopov, representing the failures of provincial education under a corrupt administration. (P02-004)

memes.label: Domestic Service
memes.description: The institution of domestic service in provincial Russia and its role in The Inspector General as a mirror of the social hierarchy. (P03-002)

memes.label: "Dramatis Personae"
memes.description: "Dramatis Personae"

memes.label: Education
memes.description: The state of education and schooling in the provincial town, overseen by the timid Luka Khlopov. (002-002)

memes.label: Educational Department
memes.description: The tsarist educational bureaucracy that oversees schools in the provincial town, represented locally by Inspector of Schools Luka Lukich Khlopov.

memes.label: Fear
memes.description: The dominant emotional state of the provincial officials upon learning of the inspector general's arrival — the play's psychological engine that drives the action, exposes corruption, and structures Gogol's comic vision. (P03-001)

memes.label: Foolishness
memes.description: The pervasive theme of foolishness and self-deception in The Inspector General, where everyone mistakes a nonentity for an authority and no one is immune from folly.

memes.label: Gossip
memes.description: The mechanism by which information—and misinformation—propagates through the provincial town, driving the entire plot of The Inspector General. (009-001)

memes.label: Governor's Office
memes.description: The municipal administrative apparatus headed by Anton Antonovich Skvoznik-Dmukhanovsky, the Governor (Gorodnichy) of the unnamed provincial town.

memes.label: The Governor's Preparations
memes.description: The Governor's frantic mobilization of officials to prepare the town for the supposed inspector's arrival, inadvertently exposing every institutional failure.

memes.label: Governor's Residence
memes.description: The Governor's house, the primary setting of The Inspector General where most of the play's action unfolds — a domestic-official hybrid that embodies the play's thesis of governance indistinguishable from private interest.

memes.label: Hospitality
memes.description: The culture of hospitality in provincial Russia and its weaponization in The Inspector General, where lavish entertainment serves to bribe and distract the supposed inspector. (033-002)

memes.label: Imperial Council
memes.description: The highest advisory body to the Tsar in imperial Russia, invoked by Khlestakov in his drunken boasting as proof of his supposed importance.

memes.label: "Imprint"
memes.description: "Imprint"

memes.label: Incognito
memes.description: The practice of anonymous official travel used by tsarist inspectors, which becomes the engine of panic and mistaken identity in Gogol's comedy. (002-002)

memes.label: The Innkeeper
memes.description: The innkeeper (Vlas) who hosts Khlestakov at the inn and threatens to stop his meals when payment is not forthcoming.

memes.label: Bribery as Systemic Corruption in The Inspector General
memes.description: An analysis of bribery in Gogol's play as a functioning parallel economy that both sustains and corrupts the tsarist administrative system — not a collection of individual moral failures but the very structure through which provincial governance operates.

memes.label: Deception and Self-Deception: The Architecture of The Inspector General
memes.description: An analysis of the play's dramatic structure as built on layered deception — Khlestakov's imposture, the officials' self-deception, and Gogol's meta-theatrical deception of the audience — with the silent scene as its culminating device.

memes.label: Social Hierarchy as Dramatic Engine
memes.description: An analysis of how the entire action of The Inspector General is driven by characters' perception of relative social rank — the plot as a chain reaction of status anxiety, with the Table of Ranks as its invisible architecture.

memes.label: Ivan Khlestakov
memes.description: A low-ranking clerk from St. Petersburg who is mistaken for the inspector general, becoming the inadvertent center of Gogol's comedy of errors. (P02-012)

relations.label: Collegiate Registrar (grade 14, the lowest rung of the Table of Ranks)

memes.label: Ivan Shpekin
memes.description: The Postmaster who habitually opens private correspondence, discovering Khlestakov's letter and exposing the imposture — a man whose private vice becomes, through the play's deepest irony, the instrument of truth. (P02-008)

memes.label: Ivanova
memes.description: The widow of a noncommissioned officer who petitions Khlestakov in Act IV, seeking redress after being flogged by the Governor. (P02-027)

memes.label: Judy Boss
memes.description: Production team member credited in colophon

memes.label: Justice
memes.description: The administration of law in the provincial town, embodied by Judge Liapkin-Tiapkin and his corrupt, negligent court.

memes.label: Khlestakov at the Inn
memes.description: Khlestakov's destitute condition at the provincial inn in Act II — hungry, penniless, and mistaken by the town officials for the incognito inspector from St. Petersburg.

memes.label: Khlestakov Courts the Governor's Family
memes.description: In Act IV Scenes XII–XIV, Khlestakov flirts first with Marya Antonovna and then, when discovered by Anna Andreyevna on his knees, seamlessly transfers his proposal to the mother, producing the play's most farcical love scene.

memes.label: Khlestakov's Boasting Scene
memes.description: The celebrated scene in Act III where Khlestakov, intoxicated by the officials' deference, invents an increasingly preposterous biography — intimate with Pushkin, author of canonical works, director of a department, and feared by the Imperial Council.

memes.label: Khlestakov's Departure
memes.description: Khlestakov's hurried departure from the town at the end of Act IV — having secured an engagement, extracted eight hundred rubles, and obtained a Persian rug, he rides away in a post-chaise just before the truth can catch up with him.

memes.label: Stepan Korobkin
memes.description: A distinguished citizen of the provincial town, one of the notables who gathers to congratulate the Governor in Act V. (P02-017)

memes.label: Korobkin's Wife
memes.description: The wife of Stepan Korobkin, listed in the Dramatis Personae as Liuliukov; she appears in Act V to congratulate the Governor's family on Marya Antonovna's engagement. (P02-019)

memes.label: Letter as Plot Device
memes.description: The intercepted letter as the climactic plot device that both triggers and resolves the mistaken-identity crisis in The Inspector General — a classic Gogolian narrative mechanism that bookends the play and delivers its devastating comic reversal. (002-002)

memes.label: The Reading of Khlestakov's Letter
memes.description: The climactic sequence in Act V where the Postmaster reads Khlestakov's intercepted letter, exposing the imposture, followed by the gendarme's announcement of the real inspector and the famous silent-scene tableau.

memes.label: Luka Lukich Khlopov
memes.description: The Inspector of Schools, a terrified bureaucrat who shakes in his boots at the mere mention of an inspector — the living embodiment of bureaucratic fear and the dysfunction of provincial education under Nicholas I. (P02-004)

memes.label: Market Square
memes.description: The commercial center of the provincial town, where merchants trade and the Governor's extortion is most visible.

memes.label: Marriage Proposal
memes.description: The marriage proposal as a comic plot device in The Inspector General, where Khlestakov's impulsive engagement to the Governor's daughter caps the play's absurdity.

memes.label: Marya Antonovna
memes.description: The Governor's daughter, briefly betrothed to Khlestakov in the play's most absurd romantic subplot — a provincial young woman whose social aspirations make her vulnerable to metropolitan deception. (P02-003)

memes.label: Merchant Class
memes.description: The merchant estate (kupechestvo) in Imperial Russia and its complex role as both victims and collaborators in Gogol's satire of provincial corruption — a social group caught between economic power and social subordination.

memes.label: Merchants
memes.description: The merchant class in the provincial town, who serve as petitioners and antagonists to the municipal administration.

memes.label: Mishka
memes.description: The Governor's household servant in The Inspector General, whose sweeping and furniture-moving punctuate the domestic chaos of the provincial administration.

memes.label: The Mistaken Identity
memes.description: The central event of the play: provincial officials mistake Ivan Khlestakov, a low-ranking clerk, for a government inspector traveling incognito.

memes.label: Municipal Administration
memes.description: The administrative apparatus headed by the Governor, encompassing all municipal functions in the provincial town. (P03-001)

memes.label: Nikolai Gogol
memes.description: Russian writer, author of The Inspector General (1836) and Dead Souls (1842)

memes.label: Nobility
memes.description: The hereditary noble estate in Imperial Russia, distinct from the service nobility created by the Table of Ranks, and its representation in The Inspector General.

memes.label: Office of Charities
memes.description: The local Board of Public Welfare administered by Artemy Zemlianika, responsible for the hospital where patients "recover like flies."

memes.label: Osip
memes.description: Khlestakov's middle-aged servant, the play's most acute observer and the only character who consistently sees through the imposture. (P02-013)

memes.label: Piotr Bobchinsky
memes.description: One of the two country squires (with Dobchinsky) who first identify Khlestakov as the inspector — the town's breathless news-bearers whose comic indistinguishability sets the entire plot in motion. (P02-009)

memes.label: Piotr Dobchinsky
memes.description: One of the two indistinguishable country squires who discover Khlestakov at the inn and set the comic plot in motion. (P02-010)

memes.label: Police Department
memes.description: The provincial police department responsible for maintaining order in the unnamed town, whose officers are more effective at creating chaos than controlling it.

memes.label: Police Force
memes.description: The municipal police force of the provincial town, operating as an instrument of the Governor's will rather than an agency of public order.

memes.label: Fevronya Poshliopkina
memes.description: The wife of a locksmith who petitions Khlestakov in Act IV, complaining that the Governor unlawfully conscripted her husband.

memes.label: Post Office
memes.description: The provincial post office where Postmaster Shpekin habitually opens private correspondence, discovering the letter that exposes Khlestakov.

memes.label: Post Office
memes.description: The post office administered by Ivan Shpekin, where private correspondence is routinely opened and the play's climactic revelation originates.

memes.label: Provincial Civil Service
memes.description: The broader tsarist provincial bureaucracy that governs the unnamed town, encompassing multiple departments and officials operating under the Table of Ranks system.

memes.label: Provincial Town
memes.description: The unnamed provincial town that serves as the setting of Gogol's The Inspector General — a microcosm of the Tsarist administrative apparatus and a stage for the play's anatomy of systemic corruption.

memes.label: The Provincial Town
memes.description: The unnamed provincial town where all the action of The Inspector General takes place — the physical and social setting of Gogol's satire, deliberately anonymous to serve as a mirror for all of Russia.

memes.label: Pugovitzyn
memes.description: A police sergeant in the provincial town, dispatched by Captain Ukhovertov to clean the streets in preparation for the inspector's arrival.

memes.label: Ivan Rastakovsky
memes.description: A retired official and "esteemed personage" of the provincial town who appears in Act V to congratulate the Governor on his daughter's engagement.

memes.label: Religious Hypocrisy
memes.description: The gap between public religious observance and private moral conduct, a recurring target of Gogol's satire in The Inspector General.

memes.label: Saratov
memes.description: A provincial government in the Russian Empire, destination of Khlestakov's purported journey from St. Petersburg to his father's estate (002-014).

memes.label: School Administration
memes.description: The educational administration of the provincial town, overseen by Inspector Luka Khlopov under the Governor's authority. (P02-004)

memes.label: Servant at the Inn
memes.description: A waiter at the provincial inn where Khlestakov is staying in Act II; the servant who delivers the landlord's ultimatum about unpaid bills.

memes.label: Servants
memes.description: The servant class in the play, represented by Osip, Mishka, and other domestic staff who navigate the social hierarchies of the provincial town.

memes.label: Social Rank
memes.description: The obsession with official rank that governs behavior and self-perception in Gogol's provincial Russia, distinct from the Table of Ranks as a formal system.

memes.label: Social Status
memes.description: The obsessive concern with social rank and status that drives the characters of The Inspector General and serves as Gogol's central psychological target — a system in which exterior position has entirely displaced interior worth.

memes.label: St. Petersburg
memes.description: The imperial capital of Russia, whose very name inspires both aspiration and terror in the provincial officials of Gogol's comedy.

memes.label: Stepan Korobkin
memes.description: A country squire and local notable in the provincial town, present at the Governor's gathering in Act V.

memes.label: Stepan Ukhovertov
memes.description: The provincial police captain, whose name ("ear-twister") reflects the casual brutality of local law enforcement in Gogol's Russia.

memes.label: Svistunov
memes.description: A police sergeant serving under Captain Ukhovertov, dispatched on various errands by the Governor and present at key crowd-control scenes. (P02-021)

memes.label: Table of Ranks
memes.description: Peter the Great's 1722 system of fourteen grades that organized the Imperial Russian civil service and defined social status in Gogol's Russia.

memes.label: "The Bribery Scene"
memes.description: "The extended comic sequence in Act IV (Scenes I–VIII) where town officials visit Khlestakov one by one to offer him bribes disguised as loans."

memes.label: "The Complaint Scene"
memes.description: "The scene in Act IV (Scenes X–XI) where merchants and townspeople petition Khlestakov with complaints against the Governor's abuses of power."

memes.label: The Courtroom
memes.description: The courtroom of Judge Ammos Fiodorovich Liapkin-Tiapkin, described as neglected and improperly maintained in Act I of The Inspector General.

memes.label: The Dining Room
memes.description: The dining room of the Governor's House, setting for a farcical scene in Act II of The Inspector General.

memes.label: The Engagement Announcement
memes.description: Khlestakov's impetuous proposal to Marya Antonovna in Act IV and the Governor's triumphant announcement of the engagement in Act V — the pinnacle of the family's delusion before the catastrophic revelation.

memes.label: The Entrance Hall
memes.description: The entrance hall of a municipal building in the provincial town, mentioned in Act I as a space degraded by official neglect in The Inspector General.

memes.label: The Gendarme
memes.description: An imperial gendarme who arrives in the final scene of the play to announce the real Inspector General's arrival.

memes.label: The Governor's Celebration
memes.description: In Act V Scenes I–VII, the Governor and his wife celebrate their daughter's engagement to the supposed inspector, dreaming of St. Petersburg and a generalship, while receiving the obsequious congratulations of the town's officials — moments before the devastating exposure.

memes.label: The Governor's Dream
memes.description: The Governor's dream of two monstrous black rats in Act I, Scene I — a superstitious omen that foreshadows the arrival of the incognito inspector and the unraveling of the town's corrupt order.

memes.label: The Governor's House
memes.description: The residence of Governor Anton Antonovich Skvoznik-Dmukhanovsky, where Khlestakov stays during Acts III-V of The Inspector General.

memes.label: The Inn
memes.description: The provincial inn where Khlestakov is stranded and where the officials first encounter the supposed inspector general, setting the entire comic plot in motion.

memes.label: The Inn Encounter
memes.description: In Act II Scene VIII, the Governor visits Khlestakov at the inn, and the mutual misunderstanding that defines the play is set in motion as each man misinterprets the other's words and intentions. (009-001)

memes.label: "The Inspector General"
memes.description: "The Inspector General — Title Page and Publication Info"

memes.label: The Kitchen
memes.description: A behind-the-scenes space mentioned in two contexts — the courthouse kitchen where the Judge stores hunting spoils, and the inn kitchen where Khlestakov observes meal preparations — both used by Gogol to reveal character through offstage domestic detail.

memes.label: "The Letter Scene"
memes.description: "The climactic scene in Act V where Postmaster Shpekin reads Khlestakov's letter to Tryapichkin, revealing the imposture to all assembled officials."

memes.label: The Opening Assembly
memes.description: In Act I Scene I, the Governor assembles the town's officials and reveals the devastating news that an Inspector General from St. Petersburg is arriving incognito with secret instructions.

memes.label: The Petition Scene
memes.description: In Act IV, the town's merchants and citizens besiege Khlestakov with petitions against the Governor's abuses, while Derzhimorda blocks the door.

memes.label: The Town Bridge
memes.description: A municipal bridge in the provincial town, used by the Governor for staging appearances and later cited as evidence of corruption in construction contracts.

memes.label: The Town Jail
memes.description: The municipal jail of the provincial town, referenced as a threat and visiting point in The Inspector General.

memes.label: Thomas Seltzer
memes.description: Translator of The Inspector General for the Project Gutenberg edition

memes.label: "Uncopyright"
memes.description: "Uncopyright"

memes.label: Vanity
memes.description: Vanity and self-regard as a driving character flaw across Gogol's provincial society. (P03-002)
